methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Realta Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $36M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private-sector comparables including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $72M implied valuation, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement to the $36M capital raised baseline. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable: while 8 same-sector private companies exist, only 1 same-stage comparable and 1 adjacent-stage comparable were identified. This narrow peer subset falls short of the minimum 3-datapoint threshold required for reliable median-based valuation, and broadening to all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable sectoral distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $61M to $83M, with a $72M median point. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties center on the thinness of exact-stage peer data in nuclear energy—a sector with limited public market history and heterogeneous technology platforms—and the absence of comparable recent Series A closings in the same subsector with which to calibrate step-up multipliers. Near-term clarity would benefit from additional Series A or Series B transactions in advanced nuclear to tighten the applicable peer set.
